question_text: A polythene rod is rubbed with a cloth and gains 240 excess electrons. Calculate the total charge gained by the rod. (The charge of one electron is 1.6 × 10⁻¹⁹ C)
correct_answer: -3.84 × 10⁻¹⁷ C or -38.4 × 10⁻¹⁸ C (negative sign indicates negative charge)
explanation: To find the total charge, multiply the number of electrons by the charge of one electron: Q = n × e = 240 × 1.6 × 10⁻¹⁹ C = 384 × 10⁻¹⁹ C = 3.84 × 10⁻¹⁷ C. Since electrons carry negative charge, the polythene rod gains a charge of -3.84 × 10⁻¹⁷ C. This is a one-step multiplication calculation applying the concept of charge quantization.
